chunks.text: 1 C.P.No.D-317 of 2020 Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D-317 of 2020 (Zulfiqar Ali Qazi v. P.O. Sindh & others) Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Hearing of Case For hearing of main case 27-05-2025 Mr. Amir Ali Bhutto, Advocate for petitioner. Mr. Manzoor Hussain Halepoto, Advocate for respondents-Town Committee Kotdiji a/w Zahid Ali Khoso, Accounts Officer, Town Committee, Kotdiji. Mr. Shahriyar I. Awan, Assistant A.G a/w Abdul Hameed, Circle Officer, ACE, Khairpur. **** Statements filed by Circle Officer, ACE, Khairpur and counsel for respondents No.3 to 5 are taken on record and copies whereof are provided to counsel for the petitioner. Heard counsel for petitioner, respondents No.3 to 5, AAG and perused the material available on record. On perusal of record, it reflects that respondents have denied the very appointment of the petitioner being their employee and releasing of salaries to him. The Circle Officer, ACE, Khairpur is present and submits that while enquiring into the matter, he came to a conclusion that the NOC purportedly issued by the Assistant Commissioner, Kotdiji and produced by the petitioner, is also a fake document. Furthermore, perusal of bank statement produced by the petitioner (available at page-27 of the petition) reflects that in the month of February, 2016, the petitioner had received two salaries and there is no record as to from where the salary amount was being 2 C.P.No.D-317 of 2020 deposited in the account of the petitioner. All these facts require evidence and such exercise cannot be undertaken by this Court under its constitutional jurisdiction.

Furthermore, perusal of bank statement produced by the petitioner (available at page-27 of the petition) reflects that in the month of February, 2016, the petitioner had received two salaries and there is no record as to from where the salary amount was being 2 C.P.No.D-317 of 2020 deposited in the account of the petitioner. All these facts require evidence and such exercise cannot be undertaken by this Court under its constitutional jurisdiction. In view of the above, instant petition being meritless is hereby dismissed leaving the petitioner at liberty to avail his remedy by other usual mode of proceedings, permissible under the law, if he desires so. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
